title: Gourmet Fried Norwegian Black Beans Fried Rice
description: A Mediterranean-inspired Norwegian dish featuring fermented black beans and a blend of fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, made in a wok with fatty and pillowy textures. This adventurous recipe combines unique flavors for a satisfying meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large wok over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the fermented black beans, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper, and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the norwegian rice to the wok, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir-fry the rice mixture for about 5 minutes, or until the rice is heated through and starting to brown.
Use a spatula to shape the rice mixture into a flat layer, then use the back of the spatula to create a series of small wells in the surface.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the bottom layer is crispy and golden brown.
Serve the gourmet fried norwegian black beans fried rice hot, garnished with chopped scallions or parsley if desired.
